Kaushik About Faculty Education and Qualification Work Experience Research Details PhD Supervision Publications Projects Testing & Consultancy Other Details Latest News & Events National Science Day 2025 19-02-2025 LECTURES: INNOVATION TO SOCIETY 05-04-2024 NATIONAL SCIENCE DAY CELEBRATIONS-2023 05-04-2024 IPR awareness program 08-09-2022 View More Dr. Vipin K. Kaushik Designation: Assistant Professor Employee ID: 1400676 Qualification: PhD (Appl. Phys.), M.Sc (Physics), MTech (Optoelctronics), CSIR-NET :0731-2582 437 / 441 : vipin[at]sgsits.ac.in, gs140676[at]sgsitsindore.in : Detailed Profile About Faculty: Dr.Kaushik is working as Assistant Professor in the Department of Applied Physics & Optoelectronics at SGSITS, Indore, since last thirteen years. He obtained M.Sc. Physics from CCS university Meerut, M.Tech.(Optoelectronics) from RGPV, Bhopal and Ph.D. in Applied Physics from DAVV, Indore. His research interest includes transparent conducting oxide thin films fabrication and characterization for their application in thin film device fabrication like diode and thin film transistors. Dr. Kaushik has supervised more than 30 PG students for their projects and published nearly 10 peer reviewed journal and conference papers. He has revied 8 journal paper for the publication in renowned international journals. Education and Qualification: PhD. (Applied Physics) awarded in April 2017 from Devi Ahilya Vishwavidyalaya (DAVV), Indore. As per UGC regulation 2009, completed PhD course work (Applied Physics, Faculty of Engineering), at IET, DAVV, Indore, during Jan-May 2013. CSIR-NET JUNE 2013 qualified for Lectureship in physical science. M.Tech. (Optoelectronics 2006-08, Specialization in Optical Communication) from Shri GS Institute of Technology & Science, Indore, affiliated to Rajeev Gandhi ProdhogikiVishwavidyalaya, Bhopal. M.Sc. (Physics 2002-2004, Specialization in Electronics) from DAV (PG) College, Muzaffar Nagar, Uttar Pradesh, affiliated to Ch. Charan Singh University, Meerut. B.Sc. (PCM 1999-2002) from DAV (PG) College Muzaffar Nagar, Uttar Pradesh, affiliated to Ch. Charan Singh University, Meerut. Work Experience: S.No. Employer Designation Department Duration of Employment-In Months Date of Joining Mode of Employment 1 Shri G S Institute of Technology and Science, Indore Assistant Professor Applied Physics 34 Regular Research Details: The research work of Dr. Kaushik centred around the growth and material characterization of pure and doped transparent conducting oxide (TCO) like ZnO thin films and reveal their properties of as a highly efficient, low-cost TCO material. Correlation between the technological parameters and material properties have been studied for ZnO based thin films. His research contains the perspective and applicability of aerosol-assisted CVD technique for the cost-effective fabrication of polycrystalline TCO thin films. The possible extension of the work is to obtain small-sized structures of thin film transistors, diodes extended to the THz antenna fabricated via the homemade aerosol assisted CVD system. PhD Supervision: NIL Publications: International/National Journal Publications (i) Vipin K. Kaushik, Tapas Ganguli, R. Kumar, C. Mukherjee and P. K. Sen, ”Growth and characterization of ZnO and MgxZn1-xO thin films by aerosol assisted chemical vapor deposition technique”, Thin Solid Films, 520, 3505- 3509 (2012). ISSN No. 0040-6090. (ii) Akash Shukla, Vipin K. Kaushik and Dixit Prasher, ”Growth and characterization of MgxZn1-xO thin films by aerosol-assisted chemical vapor deposition (AA-CVD)”, Electronic Material Letters, 10(1), 61-65 (2014). ISSN No. 1738-8090. (iii) Vipin K. Kaushik, C. Mukherjee, Tapas Ganguli and P. K. Sen,” Material characterizations of Al:ZnO thin films grown by aerosol assisted chemical vapour deposition”, Journal of Alloys and Compounds, 689, 1028-1036 (2016). ISSN No. 0953-8388. (iv) Vipin K. Kaushik, C. Mukherjee, Tapas Ganguli and P. K. Sen,” Electrical and optical characteristics of aerosol assisted CVD grown ZnO based thin film diode and transistor”, Journal of Alloys and Compounds, 696, 727-735 (2017). ISSN No. 0953-8388. (v) Vipin K. Kaushik, C. Mukherjee, Tapas Ganguli and P. K. Sen,” Electrical and optical characteristics of aerosol assisted CVD grown ZnO based thin film diode and transistor”, Journal of Alloys and Compounds, 696, 727-735 (2017). ISSN No. 0925-8388. (II) International/National Conference Publications (i) Vipin K. Kaushik, A. Shukla, R. Trivedi, T. Ganguli and P. K. Sen, “Optical Characterization of Zinc Oxide Films Grown in Indigenously Developed Aerosol Assisted Chemical Vapour Deposition (AA-CVD) System, Proceedings of National Laser Symposium-08, 7-10 Jan. 2009, LSTEC, Delhi. (ii) Vipin K. Kaushik ” Characterization of Semiconductor Thin Films Deposited by Indigenously Designed Aerosol Assisted Chemical Vapor Deposition System”, Proceedings of M. P. Young Scientist Congress, 28 Feb 2013 to 01 Mar, 2013, MPCST, Bhopal. (iii) Vipin K. Kaushik, D. Gwalvanshi, S. S. Kaushal, and P. K. Sen, ”Experimental Study of Electrical and Optical Properties of Pure and Doped ZnO Thin Films”, Proceedings of 23rd National Laser Symposium, 3-6 Dec 2014, SVM University, Tirupati. (iv) Krutika Natu, Vipin K. Kaushik, and P. K. Sen, ”Fabrication and characterization of ZnO based thin film diode”, Proceedings of 23rd National Laser Symposium, 3-6 Dec 2014, SVM University, Tirupati.
